core_mechanism: recovering a system's dissipative/transport coefficient from the low-frequency limit of the spectral density (imaginary part) of a two-point correlation function of the relevant fluctuating quantity — a Kubo-type linear-response / fluctuation-dissipation relation
brief_reason: The paper computes the spectral function of the stress-tensor-trace correlator in two analytic regimes and reads the bulk viscosity off its narrow low-frequency peak, the standard route of obtaining a transport coefficient from the zero-frequency behavior of a correlator's spectral density, with the ill-posed analytic continuation from the Euclidean correlation function to the real-frequency spectral function noted as the accompanying difficulty.
